community.title: Get community profile metrics

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

community.descriptionHTML:
Returns all community profile metrics for a repository. The repository cannot be a fork.

The returned metrics include an overall health score, the repository description, the presence of documentation, the detected code of conduct, the detected license, and the presence of ISSUE_TEMPLATE, PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE, README, and CONTRIBUTING files.

The health_percentage score is defined as a percentage of how many of the recommended community health files are present. For more information, see "About community profiles for public repositories."

content_reports_enabled is only returned for organization-owned repositories.

statistics.title: Get the weekly commit activity

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

statistics.descriptionHTML:
Returns a weekly aggregate of the number of additions and deletions pushed to a repository.

Note

This endpoint can only be used for repositories with fewer than 10,000 commits. If the repository contains 10,000 or more commits, a 422 status code will be returned.

response.description: Returns a weekly aggregate of the number of additions and deletions pushed to a repository.

statusCodes.description: Returns a weekly aggregate of the number of additions and deletions pushed to a repository.
A header with no content is returned.

statistics.title: Get the last year of commit activity

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

statistics.descriptionHTML: Returns the last year of commit activity grouped by week. The days array is a group of commits per day, starting on Sunday.

statusCodes.description: A header with no content is returned.

statistics.title: Get all contributor commit activity

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

statistics.descriptionHTML:
Returns the total number of commits authored by the contributor. In addition, the response includes a Weekly Hash (weeks array) with the following information:

  • w - Start of the week, given as a Unix timestamp.
  • a - Number of additions
  • d - Number of deletions
  • c - Number of commits

Note

This endpoint will return 0 values for all addition and deletion counts in repositories with 10,000 or more commits.

statusCodes.description: A header with no content is returned.

statistics.title: Get the weekly commit count

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

statistics.descriptionHTML:
Returns the total commit counts for the owner and total commit counts in all. all is everyone combined, including the owner in the last 52 weeks. If you'd like to get the commit counts for non-owners, you can subtract owner from all.

The array order is oldest week (index 0) to most recent week.

The most recent week is seven days ago at UTC midnight to today at UTC midnight.

response.description: The array order is oldest week (index 0) to most recent week.

statusCodes.description: The array order is oldest week (index 0) to most recent week.

statistics.title: Get the hourly commit count for each day

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

statistics.descriptionHTML:
Each array contains the day number, hour number, and number of commits:

  • 0-6: Sunday - Saturday
  • 0-23: Hour of day
  • Number of commits

For example, [2, 14, 25] indicates that there were 25 total commits, during the 2:00pm hour on Tuesdays. All times are based on the time zone of individual commits.

response.description: For example, [2, 14, 25] indicates that there were 25 total commits, during the 2:00pm hour on Tuesdays. All times are based on the time zone of individual commits.

statusCodes.description: For example, [2, 14, 25] indicates that there were 25 total commits, during the 2:00pm hour on Tuesdays. All times are based on the time zone of individual commits.
A header with no content is returned.

traffic.title: Get repository clones

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: per
parameters.description: The time frame to display results for.

traffic.descriptionHTML: Get the total number of clones and breakdown per day or week for the last 14 days. Timestamps are aligned to UTC midnight of the beginning of the day or week. Week begins on Monday.

parameters.name: owner
parameters.description: The account owner of the repository. The name is not case sensitive.

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: The name of the repository without the .git extension. The name is not case sensitive.

traffic.descriptionHTML: Get the top 10 popular contents over the last 14 days.

example.title: github/hubot: A customizable life embetterment robot.
